title: Fix silent error handling in dashboard cards and WiFiHeatmapToolVM
description: P0 bug: ISPHealthCard.swift:147 has empty catch {} that swallows ISP errors (card shows dashes). ConnectivityCard.swift:54 uses try? discarding errors. WiFiHeatmapToolVM saveSurveys/loadSurveys use try? silently losing data. Fix all three: add errorMessage state, surface errors to users. Write tests verifying errors are surfaced.
close_reason: Fixed silent error handling: ISPHealthCard empty catch replaced with errorMessage state + UI, ConnectivityCard try? replaced with do/catch + loadError display, WiFiHeatmapToolVM try? replaced with persistenceError in loadSurveys/saveSurveys. DashboardErrorSurfacingTests verifies all paths.

title: Add NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription and NSBonjourServices to macOS Info.plist
description: macOS target is missing NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription (required for NWListener/Bonjour companion service on macOS 15+) and NSBonjourServices (should declare _netmon._tcp). Without these, companion service may silently fail or app may be rejected.
close_reason: Added NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription and NSBonjourServices (_netmon._tcp) to macOS Info.plist in project.yml. Build verified.

title: ShellPingParser: statsLineWithRttPrefixInsteadOfRoundTrip fails — mdev vs stddev mismatch
description: Pre-existing failure in ShellPingParserExtendedTests.swift:149-150. Test uses 'rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 5.0/5.0/5.0/0.0 ms' but ShellPingService.statsPattern only matches 'min/avg/max/stddev = ...'. macOS ping uses 'round-trip ... stddev'; Linux ping uses 'rtt ... mdev'. Fix options: [x] Option A: change test data from mdev→stddev (if parser is macOS-only) [x] Option B: update parser to accept both stddev and mdev (better cross-platform support) Failing: result.minLatency→0.0 expected 5.0, result.avgLatency→0.0 expected 5.0. Pre-existing since d365c86.
close_reason: Fixed statsPattern regex to accept both stddev (macOS) and mdev (Linux) in ping stats output

title: CoreData migration failure: LocalDevice.isGateway requires migration policy or defaultValue
description: SwiftData migration fails on launch when upgrading from a store without LocalDevice.isGateway. Error: 'Validation error missing attribute values on mandatory destination attribute (entity=LocalDevice, attribute=isGateway)'. Store: ~/Library/Containers/com.netmonitor.NetMonitor/Data/Library/Application Support/default.store. Reproduces in test host and likely in production upgrades. Fix: add @Attribute(defaultValue: false) to isGateway, or define a VersionedSchema migration plan with a mapping policy that sets isGateway=false for existing rows. isGateway added in fe95dc5.
close_reason: Added default values (false) to isGateway and supportsWakeOnLan stored properties for SwiftData migration compatibility
